Piper pedunculatum is a plant species that belongs to the Piperaceae family, commonly known as the pepper family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in South America, for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Piper pedunculatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
